verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is a primarily cytoplasmic/nuclear chaperone that reaches the outer face of the plasma membrane under specific conditions—particularly stress states and in tumor cells. It achieves surface localization by its own mechanism: direct selective binding to negatively charged lipids (phosphatidylserine, phosphoinositides) on the PM, enabling stable membrane anchorage and oligomerization. The surface pool is documented via cell surface biotinylation, confocal microscopy, and flow cytometry. This is genuine surface accessibility (not transient recruitment), enabling immune recognition and potential therapeutic targeting.
